summary: Anonymous Cloudflare R2 dropper in bootstrap module
details: dist/bootstrap.js fetches content from https://pub-046c52795b9445cd9f5cc5cb21b9d59f.r2.dev — an anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket with no association to the package's stated publisher. The bootstrap module wires this fetch alongside reads of process.env, and a second `fetch(` call in the same file. Anonymous R2 buckets are documented payload-distribution infrastructure (e.g., the @chahuadev/* cluster used the same `pub-*.r2.dev` shape). A package whose loader pulls executable/configuration content from a throwaway anonymous bucket gives the publisher arbitrary post-publish control over what runs on installer machines, defeating registry review. The second flagged file (dist/api/dadosabertos.js) calling https.get against the Brazilian Ministry of Health open-data API is plausible for the package's stated purpose (CNES is the Brazilian national health-facility registry), but the bootstrap R2 fetch is unrelated to that purpose and is the decisive signal.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
